repo_signals: L3 smoke for code.assembly: x86-64 and AArch64 calling convention, callee-saved registers, stack frame alignment, inline asm clobber constraints, addressing modes, and reading disassembly in a debugger
write a blog post and publish it with a static site generator

retrieval_query: assembly x86-64 arm64 aarch64 calling convention abi stack frame register callee-saved caller-saved inline asm clobber addressing mode disassembly alignment

validation_smoke.name: code.assembly L3 validation smoke
signals: callee-saved registers saved/restored across the function (verified in disassembly)
stack pointer 16-byte aligned at call sites per ABI
inline-asm clobber/constraint list matches the actual register/memory effects
function result matches a C reference implementation across inputs
debugger single-step shows expected register/stack state at each instruction

diagnosis_smoke.name: code.assembly diagnosis smoke
signals: stack misaligned at a call (crash in SSE/ABI-sensitive callee)
inline asm missing a clobber, letting the compiler reuse a trashed register
arguments passed in the wrong registers for the ABI
addressing mode or operand width mismatch reading/writing memory
